ops.title: codex-ask in-repo lane guard is now path-based (cwd-independent)
ops.body:
Incident: a codex finder passed its repo clone as `-s`, and `codex-q-*`/`codex-r-*` artifacts landed in the clone. Root cause: `rejectOutsideScratch` compared the path against `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` run in codex-ask's own cwd, so a lane inside a *different* repo passed, and any git error made the check silently skip.

Fix (plugins/codex/registry.go): `repoToplevelOf` — a lexical ancestor walk of the absolute path for a `.git` entry (dir, or a worktree/submodule's file), stopping before `$HOME` so a version-controlled home dir doesn't reject the default runs base `~/.cache/codex-ask/runs`. No git subprocess, so it cannot fail open. Shared by `-s`, `CODEX_ASK_RUNS_DIR`, `--collect`, `--watch`. Deliberate limits, reviewed and upheld: symlinks stay lexical (matches the old raw-prefix behavior); bare repos and a repo rooted at `/` are not detected (flagging `/` would classify every absolute path in-repo and brick the tool). Pre-existing in-repo lanes from old binaries now refuse `--collect`/`--watch` — intended surfacing. Relates to doc 3727437 (lanes come only from `--mint-root`).
